findings.evidence: PR lines 144-150 state mutual inverse but only prove `ofComplexModule V = J`; Mathlib has `Complex.coe_smul` at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Complex/Module.lean:195` and decomposition `Complex.re_add_im` at `Mathlib/Data/Complex/Basic.lean:263`, which give the missing converse from `z = z.re + z.im * I`.
findings.fix: Add a general lemma such as `letI := (ofComplexModule V).complexModule; z • v = z • v` for the original complex action, or an equality of module structures if feasible, using `Complex.re_add_im`, `Complex.coe_smul`, and `smul_smul`.
findings.issue: `ofComplexModule_complexModule` proves only `J → Module ℂ V → J`, while the file presents the constructions as mutually inverse; the natural correspondence also needs the converse scalar-action statement for any `[Module ℂ V] [IsScalarTower ℝ ℂ V]`.

summary: The forward construction is at the right level, but the inverse API stops at the special round trip from an almost complex structure and does not state the converse for an arbitrary compatible complex module.
